programme.title: Duck Camp Dinners
programme.desc: Tune in as Jean-Paul and the crew share their first day full day at duck camp with a morning in the duck blind and catfish on the jug line. At the end of the day they put their Cajun grocery goods together for an epic duck camp meal.

programme.title: Duck Camp Dinners
programme.desc: After a morning on the Bayou, the Duck Camp Dinners crew enjoy a fresh cooked dinner while discussing the changing coastal wetlands of Louisiana. Jean-Paul gets to finally a hunt an area he had only heard about as a young man, and takes in the beauty of the live cypress trees this camp is known for.

programme.title: Duck Camp Dinners
programme.desc: After a late night of good times, venison chili, and some old school roux the crew heads out for another day of hunting ducks. Adaptability is critical, and after a couple of quick adjustments the team finds themselves back in the ducks.

programme.title: Duck Camp Dinners
programme.desc: Whether you call them Pouldeau, American Coot, or Water Chickens, chances are you don’t consider them a delicacy. However, Jean-Paul is out to prove that these abundant birds have a place at the dinner table. Tune in as thew crew makes some incredible gumbo while preparing for their last hunt at Duck Camp.

programme.title: Duck Camp Dinners
programme.desc: It's the final day at Duck Camp for Jean-Paul and the guys. They finish up their trip with a morning of hunting followed by an afternoon filled with crawfish and catfish. The trip ends on dry land and the crew reflects on what makes the duck camp so special.

programme.title: Duck Camp Dinners
programme.desc: The DCD crew spends a day on the water fishing in an effort to complete the trifecta of a meat haul. Blake shows us where he spent much of his childhood and we get a glimpse of mother natures impact on community.

programme.title: Primal Divide
programme.desc: Not my first trip to Texas, but my first chance to get a Texas buck did not disappoint on this trip. What started off as just a waterfowl photography shoot turned into an opportunity for me to grab a buck tag/license. No fence, low fence, or high fence…. Texas has some great hunting! There isn’t too many warm whitetail hunts that can be me excited but bonus hunts will get me every time!

programme.title: Primal Divide
programme.desc: When Under Armour became the Title Sponsor of the Tactical Games, well…. I actually learned what the Tactical Games were. Kinda cross fit, kinda strong man all mixed with AR and pistol shooting. Ok, Sign me up!! But what did I just sign up for?! I was about to find out!

programme.title: Primal Divide
programme.desc: John heads up to Saskatchewan to chase whitetails with BBD Outfitters. After some luck early in the week, an opportunity of a lifetime to take a moose is just a trigger pull away! John’s friend Leslie, former Marine, and catfish angler is up to bat first as she checks off a whitetail buck and moose herself in this loaded episode! You don’t know cold until it hits negative 33.

programme.title: Primal Divide
programme.desc: With the Iowa Late Muzzleloader season almost over and a thick snow covering, hopes of filling “one more tag” were up in the air. A second chance at redemption was offered on an awesome 6.5 year old Iowa buck. Cold temps, food plots, and a favorable wind can be just the things needed to fill a late season tag.

programme.title: Primal Divide
programme.desc: In most cases, the cameraman or woman hunts as well… Often their “play time” happens between scheduled season hunts for the show. But why don’t we see those? This year, I made it a point to film all of my camera girl’s hunts and decided to make an episode out of it. Without help, none of this possible! So to all the camera operators, behind the scenes industry folks… Thank you and this is for you!!!

programme.title: Primal Divide
programme.desc: A last minute trip was added to the schedule when an opportunity to join the Rise Fishing Co crew in Andros Island for Bonefish and Barracuda. I started fly fishing a few years ago but this is the first time out in the open and dealing with ocean wind for the elusive Bones! Of all forms of fishing, this is for sure the closest thing I’ve matched to spot/stalk archery.

programme.title: Primal Divide
programme.desc: Spring turkey is here! John sets his sights on chasing gobblers in Iowa and after notching the first tag, it’s time to head back to Kentucky. John grew up in the Bluegrass state and doesn’t get to hunt or visit as much as he’d like but after this season, he’s reminded why it’s important to return back home and keep that hunting camaraderie alive and well!

programme.title: Primal Divide
programme.desc: After receiving an invite to hunt mule deer in Colorado from a buddy, I jumped at the opportunity. My first time ever hunting Colorado did not disappoint. Spot/stalk on the ground with a bow will always be the most challenging, humbling, and sometimes rewarding ways to hunt. My reward... a first ever velvet Colorado Mule Deer!

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.sub-title: "North America Deer Slam- Carmen Mountain Whitetail "
programme.desc: Mark Peterson is south of the border in Mexico going after the elusive Carmen Mountain Whitetail. Known as the cousin to Coues deer, the Carmen Mountain Whitetail is small in stature but aggressive by nature. With the rut heating up Mark finds himself high on a glassing knob trying to make a move on a shooter buck.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: In this episode, Mark Peterson chases after a Red Stag, Black Buck and Fallow deer. There’s no shortage of action in this video. Argentina's borders were closed during the peak of COVID, so these animals have not been hunted for two years, which led to a game rich environment and excellent trophy quality. On this adventure, Mark is joined by his dad Earl, brother Aaron and nephew Alec to chase some of the biggest free-range Red Stags in the world. Stay tuned for more on this family adventure in Argentina.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.sub-title: "North America Deer Slam- Oregon Columbia Whitetail "
programme.desc: This week, Mark Peterson is in Oregon chasing the Columbia Whitetail. Found in only two counties of Oregon, the Columbia Whitetail is one of the most sought-after deer species. The first day posed its challenges with heavy fog at daybreak, but Mark and his guide Rob kept the pursuit for a big Columbia Whitetail. After putting on many miles throughout the morning, the fog began to lift and they finally laid eyes on their target buck. Moving into position had its challenges with plenty of satellite deer around, but in the end it came together. As an added bonus, Mark’s buck is the new Muzzleloader world record Columbia Whitetail.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.sub-title: "North America Deer Slam- Northwestern Whitetails in Saskatchewan"
programme.desc: It's early November and Mark Peterson heads north of the border to Saskatchewan to chase down his Northwestern whitetail. Unseasonably cold temps are staring Mark in the face and it has the deer hunkered down for a day and a half, but the power of food prevailed. On day two, with only 8 minutes left of shooting light, Mark's target buck showed up.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.sub-title: "Cinnamon Teal Everywhere- First Hunt In Peru | , South American Waterfowl Slam"
programme.desc: Mark's quest for the South America Waterfowl Slam has now taken him to Peru where he finds a surprising density of Cinnamon Teal. Although he already checked this species off the list in Argentina, his first hunt in Peru proves to be one of the best Cinnamon Teal shoots of his life. This is only the first afternoon and there are 5 days left to hunt the remaining 7 species.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.sub-title: "North America Deer Slam- Plains Whitetail in Colorado"
programme.desc: Mark Peterson continues on his Deer Slam and takes to the state of Colorado for the Plains Whitetail. Not often do you see more Moose than Whitetail, but after many miles on his Meindl Boots he finds a good buck bedded. A short stalk leads to quite a surprising walk-up.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.sub-title: "North America Deer Slam- California Mule Deer on Catalina Island"
programme.desc: Join Mark Peterson as he continues on his journey for the North America Deer Slam. Next up on the list, he sets his sights on the California Mule Deer on Catalina Island. The boat ride to Catalina Island itself would be an adventure, seeing a ton of sea life along the way. Find out if Mark and another hunter in camp will succeed or if the island will prove itself to be a formidable opponent.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.sub-title: "North America Deer Slam- California Sambar On Hearst Ranch"
programme.desc: Mark Peterson continues his journey for the North America Deer Slam. Next up on the list, a California Sambar. Sambar originated from Asia and were brought over to the Hearst Ranch in the early 1920s. After a fire wiped out the enclosure, they decided to let the animals roam the 80,000-acre estate. With proper management and selective harvests, these Sambar have thrived for the last 95 years.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Mark Peterson is south of the border in Veracruz, Mexico going after a Mexican Gulf Coast Whitetail. When the whitetail action slowed down, Mark tried his luck at a spot-and stalk hunt for White-lipped Peccary. With hot temps, Mark and company try to find water that the Peccary will be targeting to stay hydrated.

programme.title: Beyond The Surface
programme.desc: Join Captain Danny Allen and Hall of Fame winner Leiza Fitzgerald of CCA Florida Star as the 2 veteran anglers mix tactics and tackle ideas in the Steinhatchee flats! Join in while they fish through some foggy cool weather and teach transitional ways to consistently put fish in the boat while sharing some CCA Star Tournament details for 2024!!

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Capt Justin Leake and Meredith McCord divert attention from what’s not happening with the tarpon bite to using CoastMaster, light tackle and experience to find snapper success on heavily fished public spots.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Deer season — the sights, the sounds and the smells stick with you. It’s about remembering those who came before us and setting the right example for those who look up to us. It’s about protecting the tradition, so they always have something to look forward to. When communities come together, we see the real reason for this special time of year.

programme.title: HUnting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode, Slade and his brother Tai take us around a new investment property - a 55 acre tract in Wilkinson County, MS and go over what it took to take an undesirable raw tract, to a place with multiple offers on the first day it was put on the market.

programme.title: HUnting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode Slade takes Bentley on his very first duck hunt. Slade also gets to take a trip to duck hunting paradise with some great buddies of his. This episode is all about having a great time with great friends.
Brady Snyder hunts down a giant Iowa buck on public land.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Rifle season is off to a slow start but things are heating up with lots of tags to fill.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: With the King of the Raven in full swing, Randy decides to have a doe challenge to close out the season. With a bunch of buddies coming into camp this next week is about to be full of Surprises.

programme.title: Realtree Roadtrips
programme.sub-title: Road Trips - "Chipper Jones Scores During Nebraska's Deer Season Opener"
programme.desc: After a shift in the wind direction, everyone settles into new locations. The highly anticipated opening day does not disappoint, though. Chipper Jones makes the right move and gets rewarded when a beautiful 10-pointer (that's fresh out of velvet) gets up and walks his way.

programme.title: Wentz Brother's Outdoors
programme.desc: Zach and Carson Wentz head home to North Dakota to chase geese, ducks, and pheasants.

programme.title: Wentz Brother's Outdoors
programme.desc: Zach heads back to the homeland to chase ducks and geese in the prairie potholes.

programme.title: Turkey Posse
programme.sub-title: "Turkey Hunting the Deep South - Highs and Lows"
programme.desc: Turkey hunting in the Deep South is infamous for hard-to-hunt gobblers. The Turkey POSSE heads to Mississippi where they get schooled and can’t scratch out a gobbler to save their lives. They move over to Georgia for opening day and continue to struggle, but eventually a lone gobbler caves and slips into range for the Savage Renegauge. This hunt also brings us on a flashback to much more promising turkey hunting in Minnesota, where the POSSE runs into a pile of northern longbeards.

programme.title: Browning Films
programme.desc: Late season goose hunting in Kansas with Slade Northwest.

programme.title: Goin' Coastal
programme.desc: In the Pilot episode of Goin’ Coastal High School sweethearts Josh and Anna Calhoon hit their local waters of Ft. Walton Beach, FL and show off the diversity of the Estuary.

programme.title: Modern Hunter
programme.desc: In our pilot episode of Modern hunter, Brad Rucks heads to Saskatchewan for his first ever Canada black bear hunt.

programme.title: Realtree Roadtrips
programme.sub-title: Road Trips - "Chipper Jones Scores During Nebraska's Deer Season Opener"
programme.desc: After a shift in the wind direction, everyone settles into new locations. The highly anticipated opening day does not disappoint, though. Chipper Jones makes the right move and gets rewarded when a beautiful 10-pointer (that's fresh out of velvet) gets up and walks his way.

programme.title: Realtree Roadtrips
programme.sub-title: Road Trips - "Getting Close to Giant White-Tailed Deer and Big Bull Elk"
programme.desc: It's a couple days into the season, and the guys adjust get closer to these big Nebraska bucks. Tyler Jordan and Riley Green both have great hunts. Chipper Jones has a surprise encounter while trying to fill a doe tag.

programme.title: Realtree Roadtrips
programme.sub-title: Road Trips - "Cameraman Turned Bowhunter | Brown's Biggest Buck Ever"
programme.desc: Tyler Jordan and Bryan Brown have been playing cat and mouse with "Big Heavy" throughout their Nebraska trip. During some midday downtime, Brown wins a bet with Jordan on a 50-yard shot to earn himself the right to be in the hot seat. The guys continue making adjustments to get close to the buck. It results in Brown tagging his biggest buck ever.

programme.title: Realtree Roadtrips
programme.sub-title: Road Trips - "The Redemption Buck | Riley Green Goes Back to Nebraska"
programme.desc: Country music artist, Riley Green heads back to Nebraska to give it one last try on some of the target deer he was hunting a few weeks prior. It doesn’t take long before he has an opportunity at a big Nebraska shooter. Game on.

programme.title: Realtree Roadtrips
programme.sub-title: Road Trips - "GEORGIA Redemption Gobbler | Realtree Road Trips"
programme.desc: After a tough go at it in Oklahoma, Rigsby Stewart gets back to his home turf in East Georgia. This turkey gobbled his head off! Sweet redemption in the Georgia Pines!

programme.title: Realtree Roadtrips
programme.sub-title: Road Trips - "Chasing a Milk River Giant | Bowhunting Montana Whitetails"
programme.desc: Tyler Jordan heads to Montana, which is one of his favorite trips of the year. The Milk River is a very special place to the Realtree crew, and it doesn't take long before they spot a giant. Will they close the distance? Find out now.

programme.title: Realtree Roadtrips

programme.title: Realtree Roadtrips

programme.title: Car Industry: Emission Fraud
programme.desc: ‘Dieselgate’ made headlines around the world. For deliberately cheating the system, Volkswagen were made to pay a record $20 billion fine. But, as this investigation shows, they were far from the only culprits. We met the researchers who uncovered the cheat devise and investigate the dangers posed by NOx emissions.

programme.title: Cryptoqueen

programme.title: The Bitcoin Experiment
programme.desc: Following the global economic crisis, more and more people are questioning the way global markets function. Some are turning to decentralised payment systems like bitcoin in an attempt to regain control of the economy. But what is bitcoin?

programme.title: Eastern Economy
programme.desc: There is news from Silicon Valley every day. Many of our technologies come from "Think Tanks" of the western world. But there is also innovation and technological progress in the East. Whether it's space travel in Russia or the robotics pioneers of Japan, they're all trying to gain an innovative competitive edge. Follow us on a journey through the economic east.

programme.title: The Progress Of Cyborgs
programme.desc: How lives of ordinary people are changed forever by using new technology, and thus, becoming cyborgs.

programme.title: Quintessentially British
programme.desc: A feature documentary celebrating everything great and British

programme.title: Unwelcome Tourism
programme.desc: Unwelcome Tourism - Tourism drives up prices and brutalizes stores, restaurants and culture in general. In the most visited cities in Europe, there are neighborhoods from which residents are being forced out - tourists can pay rents that locals cannot afford. This is especially true in Venice and Barcelona, where people are mobilizing to demand that tourism be kept within certain limits.

programme.title: Norway and Electric Cars
programme.desc: Norway and Electric Cars - Electric cars are very popular in Norway. Although the air in this country is less polluted than in other European countries, Norwegians produce a lot of oil. So why do they invest so much in electric cars? What reasons led them to develop this type of car?

programme.title: The Oil War
programme.desc: Iconoclastic historian Andrew Bacevich delivers an anti-colonial critique of US foreign policy in the Middle East, informed by his long career in the Army.

programme.title: Planet E: Our Environment
programme.desc: Sustainable, eco-friendly, organic, green energy and climate-neutral mobility: what's behind it all? Is it just window dressing or innovative concepts? The environmental documentary series investigates, explores and provides facts.

programme.title: The Light and Shade of Fashion
programme.desc: The big names in fashion choose Ticino. They come here in ever greater numbers and have a significant impact on the local economy. Our investigation into Europe and Ticino's Fashion Valley reveals that behind the scenes they use every trick in the book to boost profits, from tax loopholes to a system of subcontracting that delegates production to Chinese Italians working in unacceptable conditions. A disillusioned look at the big brands.

programme.title: Sparkling: The Story of Champagne
programme.desc: A love letter to the joys and pleasures of champagne.

programme.title: The World's Finest Winemakers
programme.desc: Experience the journey of some of the world's finest winemakers as they dance with mother nature and take advantage of the Napa region's most epic vintage in decades.

programme.title: Living in the Future's Past
programme.desc: This film upends our way of thinking and provides original insights into our subconscious motivations, the unintended consequences, and how our fundamental nature influences our future as mankind.

programme.title: Rare Earth
programme.desc: For thousands of years, they lay dormant in the soil until suddenly, they became the driving force behind a technical revolution. Smart phones, laptops, touch screens, wind turbines, hybrid vehicles: they all need rare earth materials. A fascinating glimpse at cutting-edge research that could make our green technologies of the future even greener.

programme.title: Fashion Tycoons - EP 2
programme.desc: The success stories of three legends in the fashion industry.

programme.title: The Truth Of Low-Cost Fashion
programme.desc: The planet is being overwhelmed with clothes. Close to 56 million tonnes of clothes are sold every year. In Europe, the amount purchased has almost doubled since the year 2000.

programme.title: The Story of Maserati
programme.desc: Filmmaker Philip Selkirk examines the history of the Maserati automobile.

programme.title: The Rise and Rise of Bitcoin
programme.desc: A computer programmer becomes fascinated with the digital currency Bitcoin, and through his involvement in the Bitcoin community, we learn about the impending global impact of this amazing new technology.

programme.title: Bernard

programme.title: Bernard

programme.title: Bernard

programme.title: Bernard

programme.title: The Fixies
programme.desc: The Fixies follows the comical misadventures of Tom Thomas and his secret friendship with Simka and Nolik, the children of the Fixie family that lives in his apartment. programme.title: Brain Candy TV
programme.desc: Early learning for a brighter future! Your children will love to learn their ABCs, numbers, colors and even some math and science with the help of our action-packed vehicles including monster trucks, dump trucks, trains and more, plus our cute dog Lizzy.

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends Minis
programme.desc: Get ready to watch Talking Tom and Friends at their cutest! Mini in size, but massive in fun, the Minis check out the day-to-day world of the friends we all know and love where they do the most ordinary things in the most extraordinary ways.

programme.title: Yeti Tales
programme.desc: Join Us in Yeti Tales !

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Being an ‘only lamb in the flock’ Timmy has been used to getting his own way. Now for the first time Timmy is going to have to learn to get along with other little animals. Timmy is going to Nursery! Self-willed and naughty Timmy is going to have to learn how to share things with other youngsters and think about their feelings – not just his. Timmy is a gifted little footballer who needs to learn that it’s not enough to kick the ball – you have to pass it too. Harriet Heron and Osbourne Owl, who run the Nursery are going to have their hands full, but Timmy gets it right in the end with a little help from his friends of the furry, feathery and spikey type.

programme.title: Misha, the Purple Cat
programme.desc: Misha is a very lively, alert cat. She is very interested in investigating everything going on around her, which means she has some very entertaining adventures with her friends. Many things can happen in Misha’s world; especially exciting adventures, mysterious investigations, hilarious competitions... all with a feeling of close friendship.

programme.title: To Smurf A Thief
programme.desc: The Smurfs and Homnibus befriend an unfortunate boy who is forced to steal for his master. Soon, the evil thief is turned over to the magistrate and the boy is happy to live with Homnibus.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Curiosity brings Nosey Smurf to the lair of the Hag Sisters, who want to find the terrifying Sceptre of Yore... And Gargamel wants to cheat the two witches as he tries to destroy the Smurfs.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: A fierce wind carries the Smurfs' windmill away, with several Smurfs inside. Is Lazy Smurf really at fault or is an injustice being committed? Are the captive Smurfs going to be saved from the volcano's crater? Is Brainy going to smurf up his mouth for a moment?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Handy learns it’s better to have smurfed than not to have smurfed at all when he learns his true love is really a mermaid. Can they really live with each other forever?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Lazy dreams he is on Paradise Island and free from all the work Papa Smurf inflicts on him. His fantasy turns into a nightmare that sends him eagerly back to work once more.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Each morning, dew is deposited by water sprites on forest leaves. Wait till you see what happens when Scaredy Smurf gets hold of the dew-making magic wand...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Flighty Smurf, as his name implies, can't make his own mind up about anything. The other Smurfs take him to the forest for a survival test in which he can find his way home only by making decisions. He ends up in Balthazar's castle.

programme.title: Alarming Smurfs
programme.desc: Feeling unwanted, the Smurflings run away. They take things to remind them of home, so the Smurfs think there's a thief in their midst. Brainy tries to catch the thief by installing smurfler alarms. The alarms cause a ruckus that draws the Smurflings home, where Papa tells them they are wanted.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs take Grandpa Smurf to the King's castle so that he can meet Peewit. But what with one thing and another (especially another), the Smurfs and Peewit are taken aboard a Viking long ship. The Vikings take Peewit to be their mythical Great Warrior, the one who is going to vanquish the Storm King. Can the Smurfs help Peewit out of his quandary?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Hogatha disguises herself as the Golden Smurf Award to catch the Smurfs in order to use them as the main ingredient in her beauty cream. Vanity convinces her to try his recipe and before long, the wicked sorceress is covered with mud and sent careening over the hills into Gargamel's house.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs come to the rescue of two children and their grandfather when a mysterious stranger appears and causes their sleigh to crash. Knocking on Gargamel's door for help puts them and the Smurfs in danger...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Homnibus gives the Smurfs a dog that they name Puppy. The playful, normal-sized animal likes his new home, but Balthazar captures him, thinking that the Smurfs' magical secrets are in Puppy's collar. The Smurfs rescue the dog and Baby finds only music in the collar's locket.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: There isn't a River Smurf anymore: someone has frozen it near its source. The Smurfs go to the Emerald Empress' country to see what the trouble is, and they meet the Patchwork Bear as well as the Iron Pyrites...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Oh dear! Sassette has lost a tooth. Papa Smurf explains that if she puts her tooth under her pillow, the Mouse Fairy will bring her a present in the morning. But the next morning there is no present under the pillow. The Mouse Fairy has not been. She has been captured in one of Gargamel’s Smurf traps. And now the evil wizard is concocting a dreadful plan.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: It's not his fault. Clumsy Smurf never stops causing catastrophes, and even when Papa Smurf sends him to the forest on a single errand, he brings back a cloud of bad luck the shadow of which causes misfortune to anyone – or any Smurf – under it... Papa Smurf is going to need all his resources to find a cure: the silver lining that every dark cloud ought to have!

programme.title: Reckless Smurfs
programme.desc: This time, Gargamel's plan cannot fail. Undeterred by Azrael's derisive cackling, the evil wizard perfects a magic substance called Daredevil Dust, which is going to change all the Smurfs into rash, incautious swashbucklers. Will Papa Smurf fall into Gargamel's trap? Will their enemy catch all the Smurfs? Will this story have a happy ending?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Gargamel conjures a spell that makes Puppy think he's a cat. Papa receives a prescription from Homnibus to reverse the spell and all the Smurfs help. As Puppy becomes a dog again, Gargamel gets too close and he becomes a cat.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Brainy Smurf's done it again. This time, in front of Papa Smurf, too. He has recited a spell that changes everything to stone. Everything including himself and all the Smurfs and the Village, and the forest around for miles... Fortunately, Peewit appears....

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Brainy makes an enlarging potion that turns Hefty into human size. The big-little strong man captures Gargamel and brings him back to the Smurf Village where Papa Smurf magically causes Azrael to chase his master back to his dreary castle.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Papa Smurf organizes a crew to sail to Smurfberry Island because Gargamel has killed all of the trees except one to trap the Smurfs. Gargamel follows them, but once more fails to catch them because the Witch of Smurfberry Island sends her nose-eating seagulls after them.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The boy from the wood and Laconia, the deaf-mute elf, ask Papa Smurf to celebrate their wedding. The Smurfs are getting the party ready, following the advice of the Smurfette, when Gargamel comes to put a spoke in their wheel.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The evil Maestro's magical paints have the power to transport his models onto canvas – forever! Papa Smurf directs Painter to switch his paints and before long, Maestro is enshrined in a picture next to his dream girl, Smurfette.

programme.title: Scruple's Sweetheart
programme.desc: Brenda, the little witch, feels rejected: no-one is taking her to this year's Broomstick Ball. Oh, if she could find some nice-looking boy... Then she meets Scruple, apprentice of the evil wizard Gargamel. He's so handsome, and available too! What she does not know is that Scruple's only interest is her magic wand. What the Smurfs know is that they're going to save the day.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Brainy invites all the Smurfs to his party but kicks them out one by one when they don't have fun the way he instructs them. All the outcasts throw their own party and are gracious enough to allow Brainy to join them.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Thinking Baby Smurf is a leprechaun, two poachers kidnap him and try to get the small one to tell them where his pot of gold is hidden. Papa Smurf organizes everyone to attack the villains with Smurfberry pies so Baby can escape.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Life in the Smurf Village might be more peaceful if Puppy's behavior were a little more considerate. But the little Smurf dog is digging up Smurfette's flowers, dirtying Vanity's new wallpaper or ruining Papa Smurf's experiments. Yet Puppy's going to help the Smurfs out of a trap contrived by Gargamel... Or maybe he won't be disciplined enough...

programme.title: Tailor's Magic Needle
programme.desc: Tailor Smurf has to make an outfit for every Smurf before the Fall fashion show. Brainy Smurf wants to help him and, using Papa Smurf's book of Magic, he conjures up a magic needle that sews ten times faster than tailor... But is it really any good?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: As the Smurfs busily build a time capsule for all of their talents, Papa Smurf frantically tries to unravel an ancient Druid riddle before disaster strikes the forest.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Handy has built a very complex Smurf machine that does everything. It proves dangerous when it nearly destroys the village, but comes in useful when, with the help of the other Smurfs, it saves the young prince Gerard's kingdom from the queen aunt Imperia's dastardly intentions.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Allura the Enchantress possesses a very special magic orb whose energy, as she says, can lift spirits and inspire the uninspired! All you have to do is hold the orb between your hands... It is true the orb bestows inspiration, up to a point. But its side effects can be very dangerous, as the Smurfs find out when they become addicted to it...

programme.title: Harmony Steals The Show
programme.desc: Harmony unwittingly signs a contract with Ghost Writer for a symphony that imprisons him in a ghostly nightclub for life! Papa Smurf demands a jury trial with ghostly jurors and a judge that finds Harmony innocent and Ghost Writer guilty of plagiarism.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Gargamel and Balthazar command a giant robot to force King Gerard to hand over Clockwork Smurf. When the robot is about to destroy the castle, Clockwork's new, large, and awkward friend Bigmouth defeats the robot. Clockwork is hurt in the battle but Handy fixes him and implants a voice box so he can say "Hi"!

programme.title: The Magic Stick
programme.desc: With a borrowed recipe from Homnibus, Papa Smurf creates a magic stick that Clumsy takes and loses in the woods. Soon after, Bigmouth comes for a visit and before he eats all the smurfberries, Papa Smurf finds the magic stick and whisks the giant away.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Smurfette promises Mother Nature that if she creates a blue rose Smurfette will never cut it. But when the white rose becomes blue, Smurfette becomes white. The only way to make Smurfette blue again is for her to cut the rose. She reluctantly cuts the rose, returning nature to normalcy and learning that one cannot always keep a promise.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Curiosity brings Nosey Smurf to the lair of the Hag Sisters, who want to find the terrifying Sceptre of Yore... And Gargamel wants to cheat the two witches as he tries to destroy the Smurfs.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.sub-title: The Little Orange Horse With Gold Shoes
programme.desc: Sliding down a rainbow, a little winged horse loses a golden shoe. He meets the Smurfette and she helps him look for his lost horseshoe. They fall in love somehow, for who could be unmoved by the charm of a little orange horse... Or of a Smurfette?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs discover the spirit and joy of friendly competition when they hold their first "Smurfic Games". After a near miss with the troublesome cat Azrael, the Smurfs decide they need to get into better shape. Papa Smurf suggests that the Smurfs tone their bodies as well as sharpen their athletic abilities by inaugurating the "Smurfic Games".

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Hogatha, the repulsive witch, captures the Smurfette. She wants to steal from the little blue beauty her golden hair. Can the Smurfs save their little female companion before it is too late? Can Hogatha grow hair like Smurfette's?

programme.title: The Purple Smurfs
programme.desc: The purple fly bites Lazy and an epidemic starts that turns the little villagers into angry, purple Smurfs. Papa Smurf discovers that the pollen from a tuberose flower is an antidote and soon things are smurfy again.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: April Smurf's Day turns out to be not so funny when Gargamel dons a smurfberry bush disguise to capture the happy little Smurfs. Papa Smurf instructs Feathers to call the birds to attack the fake bush. After it is stripped, the captured Smurfs climb aboard Feathers and fly away.

programme.title: Bringing Up Bigfeet
programme.desc: Remember Bigmouth, the ogre, and his lovely wife Bignose? They have a baby now: tiny (by ogre standards) Bigfeet. As the Smurflings Snappy, Slouchy and Nat, and of course the ever-willing Sassette are not entitled to baby-sit Baby Smurf, they volunteer to watch over Bigfeet, while its parents are at the Ogre Ball. The little Smurfs will get more than what they expected...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Each morning, dew is deposited by water sprites on forest leaves. Wait till you see what happens when Scaredy Smurf gets hold of the dew-making magic wand...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Gargamel conjures a spell that makes Puppy think he's a cat. Papa receives a prescription from Homnibus to reverse the spell and all the Smurfs help. As Puppy becomes a dog again, Gargamel gets too close and he becomes a cat.

programme.title: The Village Vandal
programme.desc: Nasty wizard Gargamel has had enough of the Smurfs' permanent superiority. He decides to destroy their Village once and for all. This is why he trains a termite to ravage, demolish, devastate and raze all the houses in its path. But the effect is even worse than Gargamel imagined as the Smurfs think that their Village is being torn apart by the antics of the Smurflings.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Brainy invites all the Smurfs to his party but kicks them out one by one when they don't have fun the way he instructs them. All the outcasts throw their own party and are gracious enough to allow Brainy to join them.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Allura the Enchantress possesses a very special magic orb whose energy, as she says, can lift spirits and inspire the uninspired! All you have to do is hold the orb between your hands... It is true the orb bestows inspiration, up to a point. But its side effects can be very dangerous, as the Smurfs find out when they become addicted to it...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The Smurfs take Grandpa Smurf to the King's castle so that he can meet Peewit. But what with one thing and another (especially another), the Smurfs and Peewit are taken aboard a Viking long ship. The Vikings take Peewit to be their mythical Great Warrior, the one who is going to vanquish the Storm King. Can the Smurfs help Peewit out of his quandary?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Handy learns it’s better to have smurfed than not to have smurfed at all when he learns his true love is really a mermaid. Can they really live with each other forever?

programme.title: The Trojan Smurf
programme.desc: Gargamel, the evil wizard, has devised a new plan to capture the Smurfs: he builds a giant statue in the shape of Papa Smurf. Thus, he hopes, the Smurfs are going to drag the statue into their Village.

programme.title: Queen Smurfette
programme.desc: Smurfette becomes Queen for a day on her birthday. But this gets out of hand when Father Time falls asleep, neglecting to end the days. Tempers begin to flare among the Smurfs, who are tired of servitude. Papa rushes to wake Father Time and brings peace again to Smurfland.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Brainy Smurf is aghast. Sassette cannot read, and thus might be forever prevented from learning the pearls of wisdom collected in his book "Brainy Smurf's quotations"! He tries teaching her how to read. What she can read almost from the start is a formula to make all the books disappear! All the books in the world, including Gargamel's spells and Homnibus' books of remedies!

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: As the Smurfs prepare for the Festival of the Stars, Gargamel plants a creature in their midst that smells so rotten that he can easily be traced to the Smurf village. Papa Smurf realizes that Born Rotten is from another planet, and he instructs Feathers to return him to his home before Gargamel finds them.

programme.title: The Abominable Snowbeast
programme.desc: Brainy and his friends organize a journey to Ice Mountain to pick the rare snow flower for Papa Smurf. They meet a terrifying snow beast that befriends them, due to Smurfette's kindness, and helps them find the tiny flower.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The wonderful joy of Christmas for Baby Smurf and everyone else is almost ruined by the evil Chlorhydris who puts a spell on Mr. Nicholas to stop him from spreading holiday cheer. Fortunately, Papa Smurf and his little blue creatures break the spell and Christmas spreads over the land once more.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: It must be a coincidence: just as an elephant crosses the forest and meets baby Smurf, the Smurfs decide to create a big Smurf Circus. They are going to meet the infernal sorcerer Balthazar and Baby Smurf will have a lot of fun...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Clumsy Smurf's misadventures take him across a river into Pussywillow Hollow. There he meets the Pixies – charming and shapely beings whose enemies, the Wartmongers, will meet the Smurfs in some unforeseen ways.

programme.title: The Smurfiest Of Friends
programme.desc: Brainy Smurf had one friend: Clumsy Smurf. But poor Clumsy is so, well, clumsy, that Brainy refuses to see him again. So now he is left without anyone willing to listen to him. Maybe Buddy, the good-looking imp met by chance near the Great Oak can be a pal for Brainy....

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Brainy invites all the Smurfs to his party but kicks them out one by one when they don't have fun the way he instructs them. All the outcasts throw their own party and are gracious enough to allow Brainy to join them.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Brainy feels up to the occasion when he starts the recipe for cookies. He is sure to win the contest: he borrowed a recipe from Papa Smurf's Book of Magic. But a big surprise, and some nasty happenings, await poor Brainy... and the whole Village.

programme.title: The Mr Smurf Contest
programme.desc: When Papa decides to throw a Harvest Moon ball, all the male Smurfs compete in a "Mr Smurf" contest for the honor of escorting Sassette. Gargamel sneaks into the contest after shrinking himself with blue dye, in the hope of capturing all the Smurfs when he returns to normal size. The Smurfs kick him out and Sassette lets Papa escort her.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The wonderful joy of Christmas for Baby Smurf and everyone else is almost ruined by the evil Chlorhydris who puts a spell on Mr. Nicholas to stop him from spreading holiday cheer. Fortunately, Papa Smurf and his little blue creatures break the spell and Christmas spreads over the land once more.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: The evil wizard Gargamel spreads the forest with the germs of the uncontrollable hopping cough. Baby Smurf is the first victim and all the Village of the Smurfs goes frantic with worry... Fortunately, Papa Smurf thinks there might be a cure.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Get up early in the morning, before any other Smurf is out of bed, to prepare a scrumptious Smurfberry muffin. Spend all morning on preparing Smurf delicacies for lunch. Than all afternoon to have dinner ready – and delicious – on time. And do you get so much as a thank you form the Smurfs? So Greedy Smurf quits. The Village is in trouble... And soon, so is Greedy.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: April Smurf's Day turns out to be not so funny when Gargamel dons a smurfberry bush disguise to capture the happy little Smurfs. Papa Smurf instructs Feathers to call the birds to attack the fake bush. After it is stripped, the captured Smurfs climb aboard Feathers and fly away.

programme.title: Smurfiplication
programme.desc: The evil wizard Gargamel creates a magic formula that duplicates everything it touches. And surprise! Suddenly there are six Brainy Smurf! This creates a few interesting situations in the Village... And also at Gargamel's!

programme.title: Papa's Day Off
programme.desc: The Smurfs decide Papa needs a rest, so they agree to work out their problems without him. Papa feels obsolete but his wise friend, Homnibus, shows how the Smurfs couldn't get along without him. When Papa returns in time to fix a leaking dam, he feels useful again.

programme.title: Speak For Yourself, Farmer Smurf
programme.desc: Farmer hides his love for Smurfette by assuming a disguise and sending her poems. When they finally meet, they are captured by Bigmouth and in their struggle to get free, they discover their mutual friendship.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.sub-title: Journey To The Centre Of The Smurf
programme.desc: Winter is setting in on the Village of the Smurfs. The little Smurfs are freezing. But what is Clumsy doing with this strange rock he's discovered? And why is Miner Smurf so enthusiastic about their new discovery? Surely, one more addition to Clumsy's rock collection is not worth all this hoopla... The Smurfs will discover danger and surprises when they go underground.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Gargamel pours a glue-like potion on Clumsy and when anyone touches him, they become stuck to him. The Smurfs become linked to each other until Papa grabs Gargamel's nose. Gargamel, who is annoyed at having all the Smurfs stuck to him, devises a counter-potion and the Smurfs are free once more.

programme.title: The Great Lime Crop Failure
programme.desc: Bullrush the Wartmonger is in a panic because the lime harvest has failed. So he captures Farmer Smurf, the world’s greatest agricultural expert, to solve his problems. Farmer Smurf installs an irrigation system and saves the harvest. He then escapes in a bubble and returns to the Smurfs.

programme.title: Monster Smurfs
programme.desc: This is spook-a-Smurf eve, and all the Smurfs get ready for the fancy dress party... But Brainy Smurf gets hold of Papa Smurf's Book of Magic, and all the Smurfs are changed into hideous but funny creatures. Yet danger lurks...

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Handy learns it’s better to have smurfed than not to have smurfed at all when he learns his true love is really a mermaid. Can they really live with each other forever?

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Papa Smurf organizes a crew to sail to Smurfberry Island because Gargamel has killed all of the trees except one to trap the Smurfs. Gargamel follows them, but once more fails to catch them because the Witch of Smurfberry Island sends her nose-eating seagulls after them.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: His wicked cousin, Malcolm the Mean, who plans to kill him and mount him in his trophy room, has turned King Gerard into a black stag. The little Smurfs help their friend. When King Gerard is transformed back into his human form, Malcolm becomes imprisoned in the body of the stag.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Papa instructs adult Smurfs (Nat, Snappy and Slouchy) to deliver an hourglass to Father Time. While there, the three step inside a clock which is running backwards and they go back in time. Father Time says there is no way to make them older. All must accept them as Smurflings and learn that they have a lot to offer.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Hefty Smurf meets an old Trolless in the forest, and the old crone begs him to relieve her of a red spot on her nose by... giving her a good kick here and now! Hefty half-heartedly obliges... And the result is a big surprise for him!

programme.title: Don Smurfo
programme.desc: Everyone has their own dreams. That of Smurfette is to meet the legendary Don Smurfo, the dashing, witty, swashbuckling hero of many an engrossing tale. The Smurfs shrug at this infatuation. But when a real-life Don Smurfo appears in the village and Smurfette snubs them all in favor of the bragging stranger, they take the matter a little more seriously.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Gargamel infects Hefty's heart with hate and then uses the little Smurf as a decoy for the others. Fortunately, Hefty's love overcomes his hate and he helps his little friends escape from Gargamel's clutches.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Harmony Smurf is no longer wanted in the Smurf Choir: his singing is too far off-key. But Papa Smurf gives him an amulet that will make him a vocal virtuoso. Now here comes Hogatha...

programme.title: Adventure of mark twain
programme.desc: Restored to High Definition, recently released by Magnolia in Dec. 2012. The imagination of Mark Twain meets the magic of Will Vinton, in this fantastical journey of Tom Sawyer, Becky Thatcher and Huck Finn as they travel through time in search of Halley's Comet. This year marks the 175th Anniversary of Twain's birth, and the 125th Anniversary of Twain's pinnacle work Adventures of Huckleberry Finn, and the 100th anniversary of his death.

programme.title: Eight Days Episode 9 - Graduation day
programme.desc: Today we graduate. The guestshave a day of pampering. The get ready to leave and we hold a graduation for the guest

programme.sub-title: Making A Chef S2 Ep1 -- The Best Part of Waking Up
programme.desc: Mark visits local award-winning roaster Umble Coffee and the People's Cup Microroastery to get the lowdown.

programme.sub-title: Making A Chef S1 Ep3 -- Dont Play With Fire or Brisket

programme.desc: We will feature three easy meal prep recipes. BBQ Chicken Meal, Turkey Kebobs and Quinoa Fried Rice with sticky glazed chicken.

programme.title: Bobby's Kitchen Basics Episode 2

programme.desc: Go on a road trip with Gabriel and his good buddy Abe, from northern California to Oregon and then from San Francisco, California up north to Mendocino County.

programme.title: Gabriels California Road Trip
programme.desc: Join Gabriel driving down Highway 1 in northern California, the beautiful Pacific Coast Highway.

programme.sub-title: Making A Chef S1 Ep4 -- Not All Pies Are Sweet
programme.desc: Pizza is a favorite food in the Coblentz household. Marks dad stops in to show him how to make a few different types of pizza dough, plus give him some pointers on how to assemble a delicious homemade pizza pie.

programme.sub-title: Kings of Fi$h -- E2: Boys Will be Boys

programme.title: Gabriel in Honolulu

programme.desc: In this episode, Joe catches a break and experiences some tail winds while paddling his canoe. He and his buddy Kyle engage in some conversation.

programme.title: Joe Robinet - Adventures in the Outdoors
programme.desc: In this episode, Joe wakes up early in the morning and talks about some weird dreams he had the night before.

programme.title: Goodtaste With Tanji Episode 1
programme.desc: Head to the sensational and seasonally inspired Supper in San Antonio at the historic Pearl and then to Austin for lunch at the award-winning Odd Duck. Then, dinner is served at The Original Ninfa’s in Houston, where the fajita is said to have been created.

programme.title: Goodtaste With Tanji Episode 2
programme.desc: It’s a feast for your eyes and your taste buds at Brennans of Houston with its wingback chairs and Cajun cuisine -- at the top of it’s game for more than 40 years. Then, uptown eats in the middle of nowhere at Garden Company Marketplace & Café, along with Latin-inspired seafood from Alcomar in Austin.

programme.title: Monumental Challenge
programme.desc: The Yamuna River is both a backdrop to the world's most famous monument to love and its greatest threat.

programme.title: Monumental Challenge
programme.desc: A financial hub in China undergoes a transformation

programme.title: Air Crash Confidential
programme.desc: In an attempt to eliminate human error, airlines have increased the risk of flying

programme.title: Air Crash Confidential
programme.desc: Discover why a Jumbo Jet exploded mid-flight and DC10s fell from the sky due to fatal design errors in planes.

programme.title: The Giant of the Sky: The Making of the Airbus A380
programme.desc: This series following the story of the construction of the world's biggest and heaviest airliner, told through the people who made it happen.

programme.title: Extreme Construction: The Suez Canal
programme.desc: Explore the original construction of the Suez Canal in 1859 and look into the expansion of it.

programme.title: Extreme Construction: The Paris Metro

programme.title: Hands On Crafts for Kids
programme.desc: The earth is full of wonderful creatures. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs crafts simple animals that are easily adaptable and molds a robot mouse. Artist Franz Spohn draws creatures that are just like us – or not. Jenny uses glow-in-the-dark clay to craft a spooky-sweet charm for your backpack.

programme.title: Draw Better Portraits: Capturing Likeness
programme.desc: Learn to draw and paint with CraftsyTV.

programme.title: Sewing
programme.desc: Learn to sew with CraftsyTV.

programme.title: Scrapbook Soup
programme.desc: It's fun to take and share selfie photos. Amy Tan gets selfies out of the camera and onto artful pages. Ashley Horton uses fancy frames to capture a bit of herself in her designs.Amy Tan has a tip for adding stitches to patterned paper. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer creates a fun photo series from every day activities.

programme.title: Scrapbook Soup
programme.desc: This episode features paint and ink techniques. First, Dina Wakley uses paint and ink to create a vibrant watercolor background for her page layout. Julie interviews Joe Rotella about his glass etching project. Then, Kathy Cano Murillo tie dyes fabric to use as a background for her page layout. Last, Julie creates a tie dye look with a watercolor technique.

programme.title: Beads, Baubles & Jewels
programme.sub-title: Get Creative with Resin and Wire
programme.desc: Mixed media artist Susan Lenart Kazmer demonstrates making faux stones with glass glitter, inclusions and resin, and then she shows cold surface work to mimic enameling on metal. Today's Beading Lesson is on knotting with leather.

programme.title: Beads, Baubles & Jewels
programme.desc: Sometime all we need is a walk outside to jump start our creativity. A simple breath of fresh air clears our head and allows to ideas to form. Nature all by itself is inspiring. Today Nealay Patel uses the branches of a tree to prompt a design for a necklace. Then leaves motivate Candie Cooper to create fringed leather leaf earrings. Finally, Tammy Honaman has peyote with a twist.

programme.title: The Big Baking Experiment
programme.desc: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.

programme.title: It's Sew Easy
programme.desc: A classic style sometimes can use a change whether for fashion or fit. Fit expert Kathleen Cheetham teaches how to create a back yoke on a blouse to eliminate multiple darts or change to a more casual look. Then, it's a lesson on sleeves for your Little Black Dress with Angela Wolf. Finally, Gretchen Hirsch alters a favorite pattern by adding a flounce for a fashion flare.

programme.title: It's Sew Easy
programme.desc: The Industrial Revolution was in full swing and women ventured beyond their homes into the workplace and politics. Angela Wolf creates a luxurious kimono duster inspired by those worn over long, full skirts and dresses. Kathy McMakin uses today's machines to create a precious heirloom baby bonnet you'll treasure.

programme.title: Off Our Needles
programme.sub-title: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
programme.desc: Since when do knitters use crochet hooks? Find out how to work the provisional cast-on, cable cast-on, and more during the first Novel-T check in!

programme.title: Off Our Needles
programme.desc: Hold onto your needles! Tracie and Jodi, are here to dish out fresh fiber inspiration. From project picks to wooly good gossip, things are about to get super knitty… and maybe just a little nutty.

programme.title: Hands On Crafts for Kids
programme.desc: We're exploring shapes today. Shape is what gives an object its form -- they can be geometric like squares or triangles -- or a shape can be anamorphic or free form. We begin with a Japanese printing technique called gyotaka to make fish shaped prints. Then, we're making a homemade hula hoop. The next page of our art journal is using the shape of your fingerprint to create faces to express your mood. Finally, mix and match shapes to create monster cubes.

programme.title: Five-Course Photo
programme.desc: Learn photography with CraftsyTV.

programme.title: Creative Cake Design presented by CraftsyTV
programme.desc: Rachael Teufel demonstrates how to use an acetate template to create an underwater wrap design using buttercream icing. She shares her tips for achieving a clean design on your acetate before transferring it to your cake. This technique can be used to create any design you choose, making it a versatile method for any cake decorator.

programme.title: Creative Cake Design presented by CraftsyTV
programme.desc: Transform your cake into an adorable polar bear using modeling chocolate and fondant. Rachael Teufel shows you how to use a simple template to cut out shapes for your polar bear, as well as some basic sculpting techniques that are easy enough for beginners.

programme.title: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
programme.desc: -Cordless drills are a staple in many woodworking shops and deserve a dedicated storage stand. George Vondriska shows you how to make your own cordless drill stand, fully equipped with a power strip for easy charging, plus storage drawers for tips and bits. This project is great for practicing biscuit joinery and hand tool work.

programme.title: Fresh Quilting
programme.desc: Explore design from a fresh perspective. Free-motion quilt artist Christina Cameli echo quilts and contrasts curves and lines to create new designs. Art historian and quilter Allison Evans joins modern quilt activist Heather Grant to discuss charity quilting events. Quilt innovator Jacquie Gering quilts with gentle curves.

programme.title: The Midnight Quilt Show
programme.desc: This practical and cute quilted zipper pouch is one of Angela's favorite projects yet, because it skips the piecing and gets straight to the quilting! In this episode, she shares three quilting designs every quilter should know: ribbon candy, serpentine lines and the paisley feather, while making a small, easy quilting project for beginners that only requires two fat quarters to make!

programme.title: The Midnight Quilt Show
programme.desc: Are you a "slow and steady" quilter or more of a "forget perfect, finish fast" quilter like Angela? Tonight, she uses templates and block piecing with LOTS of triangles to create an out-of-this-world ombré quilt. Plus, she shares some handy tips for quilting with rulers.

programme.title: It's Sew Easy
programme.desc: This season features a wardrobe of athletic or everyday wear with Angela Wolf. To kick off athletic wear, Angela demonstrates a yoga top with mesh color-blocked inserts. Then, Emily Thompson has the perfect pattern for a workout skirt for kids featuring shorts underneath.

programme.title: It's Sew Easy
programme.desc: Be one of a kind when you take your favorite pattern and alter, change or rearrange the pieces for a new look. Kay Whitt is first with a wrap dress – the key to this look is choosing different fabrics for the bodice trim. Then meet Sarah Gunn, known for her tunics and altering the necklines to fit your style. We finish with Angela Wolf and a lace trimmed tank. She will be featuring all different tank alterations from a basic pattern this season.

programme.title: Beads, Baubles & Jewels
programme.desc: Bend it, twist it, shape it, wear it. Jewelry designer Sandra Lupo warms up by bending and shaping sheet metal with a tool she invented. Then, she enhances the designs with patterns, texture and color. Host and designer Katie Hacker make a quick and easy charm bracelet to cool down.

programme.title: Beads, Baubles & Jewels
programme.desc: In the hands of an artist – even the simplest materials take on a new life. We begin with stone cabochons. Then artist Ashley Bunting uses jump rings and crimp tubes to make a simple chainmaille style chain with the most basic of materials. Then Katie creates a prayer mantra bracelet inspired by ancient art. Finally, learn the basics of fine silver metal clay and torch firing with artist Tammy Honaman and complete a necklace using just a pendant and simple leather cording.

programme.title: Scrapbook Soup
programme.desc: Make the most of your stamps or create new ones. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer carves her own stamps. May Flaum makes a stamped image pop off the page. Christine Drumheller has a handy gluing tip for clips and charms. Cheryl Boglioli stamps contemporary positive and negative images using gelatin plates.

programme.title: Make It Artsy
programme.desc: Books are not just for reading! Tool expert Joe Rotella cuts a niche in a book to create a planter. Craft metal book hinges with designer Mary Hettmansperger and Mou Saha makes vintage books into colorful art journals. Host Julie Fei Fan Balzer has a tip for creating a charming little book necklace.

programme.title: Chemo Caps
programme.desc: Learn to knit with CraftsyTV.

programme.title: Chemo Caps
programme.desc: Learn to knit with CraftsyTV.

programme.title: The Midnight Quilt Show
programme.desc: Don't try this on your turkey! Learn everything you ever wanted to know about basting as Angela tackles a simple four-patch design with complex sashing.

programme.title: The Midnight Quilt Show
programme.desc: This practical and cute quilted zipper pouch is one of Angela's favorite projects yet, because it skips the piecing and gets straight to the quilting! In this episode, she shares three quilting designs every quilter should know: ribbon candy, serpentine lines and the paisley feather, while making a small, easy quilting project for beginners that only requires two fat quarters to make!

programme.title: The Midnight Quilt Show
programme.desc: This week Angela is preparing to host a dinner for quilters, so of course, she has to quilt up a few placemats!

programme.title: The Midnight Quilt Show
programme.desc: This week, Angela's quilting a stash-busting table runner that's perfect for entertaining (plus, quick to whip up!), and sharing her favorite tips for throwing the perfect party!

programme.title: Baking & Cooking
programme.desc: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.

programme.title: Slow TV
programme.desc: Slow it down with cookie decorating.

programme.title: Slow TV
programme.desc: Take it slow with this model railroad tour.

programme.title: Man About Cake
programme.desc: In this episode of Man About Cake, we learn that shiny is a color, and edible lace isn't as difficult as it looks.

programme.title: Man About Cake
programme.desc: This cake's got sole. JJR demonstrates fashion fondant techniques, piped stringwork and tops it off with a custom sugar shoe topper.

programme.title: Man About Cake
programme.desc: Marble cakes rock. In this episode our Cake Slayer demystifies the marbled fondant technique and constructs a fully marbleized cake (from the inside out!). If only granite had chocolate swirls like these.

programme.title: Man About Cake
programme.desc: Geometric cutout cakes are all the rage. JJR takes on tiled fondant and custom toppers for a modern kids' birthday cake design (sprinkles included).

programme.title: Man About Cake
programme.desc: Celebrate a love that's out of this world with Joshua John Russell's "cosmic love" illustrated cake!

programme.title: Jazz Basics 1
programme.desc: Join Nick in his first Jazz Basics class that focuses on weight shifts and working your feet through the floor. Practice using your plié as you take up space and travel through each exercise sequence.

programme.title: Jazz Basics 2
programme.desc: Nick's second Jazz Basics class covers direction changes, coordination, and staying grounded in your movement. Work on your pas de bourrées, jazz lunge, and a short phrase you will see later in Nick's jazz combination!

programme.desc: The earth is full of wonderful creatures. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs crafts simple animals that are easily adaptable and molds a robot mouse. Artist Franz Spohn draws creatures that are just like us – or not. Jenny uses glow-in-the-dark clay to craft a spooky-sweet charm for your backpack.

programme.title: Hands On Crafts for Kids
programme.desc: Imagine a world all in black and white -- it would be pretty boring. Today we're going to look at some ways to use color. The first project is an art supply holder. Brightly colored cord is knotted together to form the hanger. Batik a monster tank top using a color resist technique. Today's art journal page is an experiment on color mixing to create funky printed leaves. The color wheel is the primer for how to mix color but it creates a beautiful work of art when you add glitter for our last project.

programme.title: Cities By The Sea
programme.desc: Cities by the sea are gateways to the world. They are all intrinsically defined by their position by the sea and consequently by an openness to the world, a spirit of discovery and a desire for adventure. The geo-strategic location of these cities has turned them into chess figures of changing political powers and systems, into transit locations of dreamers and migrants, and into melting pots of people from different backgrounds and cultures. And the endlessness of their offshore waters has always been a magnet for those with desires, dreams and visions. With the curious eyes of a stranger, the series wanders through a selection of the most interesting sea cities of the world and discovers their ways and idiosyncrasies, using the very latest aerial technologies and an opulent cinematic language.

programme.title: Travel Diaries
programme.sub-title: Abbey of Fontenay - Oldest Cistercian complex
programme.desc: Abbey of Fontenay - Oldest Cistercian complex

programme.title: The World Is Yours
programme.desc: This time in "The World is Yours" will take you to a very peculiar island: Madagascar! There we will spend time in the company of the strangest creations of Nature. Caverns, beaches, reefs and exuberant forests where the most exotic fauna and flora of the planet coexist, will be the destination of this wonderful journey in which you will be received by its friendly inhabitants and their zest for life! Now, if what you are looking for is to share with friends and feel like at home, nothing like New Orleans' southern hospitality. Get ready because the food and the music are not the only charms of this moving city. History, customs and colorful festivals await you in the streets of this picturesque place. This tour ends with a trip to the past, to the age of castles and cathedrals at Edinburg! Where youll feel as the star of stories of knights in armor, you will listen to the stories of the soldiers who participated in the siege of the castle and visit the Cathedral of St. Egidio to enjoy its architecture with original stained-glass windows of the XXIV century its really worth knowing!

programme.title: Passport Heavy
programme.sub-title: Belize: The Hidden Gem of Central America
programme.desc: Feel Belize's unique energy, a blend of rich history, mouthwatering cuisine, and warm hospitality. Dive into its deep-rooted culture and savor culinary traditions intertwined with the nation's past. Belize's natural beauty boasts crystal-clear waters, lush jungles, arguably the world's largest LIVING barrier reef (following the detterioration of Australia's). It's a haven for scuba and snorkeling enthusiasts. Experience Belize's charm and natural wonders!

programme.title: Painting The Town
programme.desc: Folk artist Eric Dowdle travels across North America, meeting the local people, sampling food, learning about culture and touring local attractions in an effort to determine what makes each place unique. Each episode culminates in the creation of a piece of colorful folk art that symbolizes the place Dowdle has visited.

programme.title: The World's Greatest Cities of The World
programme.sub-title: The World's Greatest Cities of The World: Hong Kong, New York, Paris, Bangkok, Auckland, Berlin, Los Angeles
programme.desc: Your first class ticket to the cities of the world. This series features large and small cities from around the world to reveal their icons, culture, tourist and hidden highlights with information about local customs, the cities personality and history.

programme.title: Travel Thru History
programme.desc: Travel Thru History is a unique, Emmy nominated travel series that explores diverse cities and historical sites around the World while experiencing the cultural and archaeological features of these unique locations. From the oldest building in the Western Hemisphere to the famous French Quarter, a spectacular chain of islands off the Southern coast of Florida to the birthplace of California, the Travel Thru History series is a must see to learn about America's rich and fascinating history by traveling to amazing cities across the states. It explores diverse cities and historical sites while experiencing the cultural and archaeological features of these unique locations.

programme.title: Travel Diaries
programme.desc: Ajanta Caves - Maharashtra

programme.title: The World Is Yours
programme.desc: We will travel to Santorini, to build castles of red sand, or black sand, on the beach of Akrotiri. We will see how much fun Santorini offers us, along its mountain range, with its emblematic towns, although hidden on the side of a volcanic mountain, are villages that are full of activity at any time of the day. We will make a stop for tea in Hong Kong and learn about its history. With one of the largest and most iconic horizons in the world, our next destination will make you believe that the past and the present merge in perfect harmony. If you want to try something more traditional of classical Chinese cuisine, enjoy a "Dim sum", a custom similar to British "tea time". "Dim sum" literally means drinking tea. On the last leg of this trip we will explore Mexico City searching for the remains of civilizations that no longer exist The Aztec Empire. Perhaps, we will even find evidence of some that many believe that never existed. According to what is known about them, it was a civilization quite advanced and capable of supporting hundreds of thousands of people.

We will make a stop for tea in Hong Kong and learn about its history. With one of the largest and most iconic horizons in the world, our next destination will make you believe that the past and the present merge in perfect harmony. If you want to try something more traditional of classical Chinese cuisine, enjoy a "Dim sum", a custom similar to British "tea time". "Dim sum" literally means drinking tea. On the last leg of this trip we will explore Mexico City searching for the remains of civilizations that no longer exist The Aztec Empire. Perhaps, we will even find evidence of some that many believe that never existed. According to what is known about them, it was a civilization quite advanced and capable of supporting hundreds of thousands of people.

programme.title: Passport Heavy
programme.sub-title: Belize: The Hidden Gem of Central America - Part 2
programme.desc: Feel Belize's unique energy, a blend of rich history, mouthwatering cuisine, and warm hospitality. Dive into its deep-rooted culture and savor culinary traditions intertwined with the nation's past. Belize's natural beauty boasts crystal-clear waters, lush jungles, arguably the world's largest LIVING barrier reef (following the detterioration of Australia's). It's a haven for scuba and snorkeling enthusiasts. Experience Belize's charm and natural wonders!

programme.title: Painting The Town
programme.desc: Folk artist Eric Dowdle travels across North America, meeting the local people, sampling food, learning about culture and touring local attractions in an effort to determine what makes each place unique. Each episode culminates in the creation of a piece of colorful folk art that symbolizes the place Dowdle has visited.

programme.title: The World's Greatest Cities of The World
programme.sub-title: The World's Greatest Cities of The World: Sydney, Guangzhou, Singapore, London, Tokyo, Jerusalem, Toronto
programme.desc: Your first class ticket to the cities of the world. This series features large and small cities from around the world to reveal their icons, culture, tourist and hidden highlights with information about local customs, the cities personality and history.

programme.title: Cities By The Sea
programme.desc: Cities by the sea are gateways to the world. They are all intrinsically defined by their position by the sea and consequently by an openness to the world, a spirit of discovery and a desire for adventure. The geo-strategic location of these cities has turned them into chess figures of changing political powers and systems, into transit locations of dreamers and migrants, and into melting pots of people from different backgrounds and cultures. And the endlessness of their offshore waters has always been a magnet for those with desires, dreams and visions. With the curious eyes of a stranger, the series wanders through a selection of the most interesting sea cities of the world and discovers their ways and idiosyncrasies, using the very latest aerial technologies and an opulent cinematic language.
